  "text": "HTTP/1.1 to HTTP/2 header translation in Envoy is handled across multiple C++ source files with specific responsibilities:\n\n## Header Case Normalization (Lowercase)\nIn `source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc`, the `ConnectionImpl::completeCurrentHeader()` method (line 565) performs header case normalization by converting all HTTP/1.1 header names to lowercase using `current_header_field_.inlineTransform([](char c) { return absl::ascii_tolower(c); });`. Set via `headers.setPath()` at line 1159 from the parsed URL path\n2. **:scheme** - Set via `headers.setScheme()` at line 1145 with lowercase conversion using `absl::AsciiStrToLower()`\n3. **:authority** - Converts HTTP/1.1 upgrade requests to HTTP/2 format\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeResponseFromH1toH2()` (line 1065) - Converts HTTP/1.1 upgrade responses to HTTP/2 format\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeRequestFromH2toH1()` (line 1080) - Reverse transformation from HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1\n- `Utility::transformUpgradeResponseFromH2toH1()` (line 1093) - Reverse response transformation\n\nThese transformations replace the CONNECT method, remove upgrade-related headers, and handle the protocol pseudo-header.\n\n## HTTP/2 Codec Integration\nIn `source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc`, the `ConnectionImpl::ClientStreamImpl::encodeHeaders()` method (line 274) and server variant call the upgrade transformation functions when encoding HTTP/1.1 upgrade requests for transmission as HTTP/2.\n\n## Header Formatting\nIn `source/common/http/http1/header_formatter.h`, the `ProperCaseHeaderKeyFormatter` class handles optional header key formatting for HTTP/1.1 output, allowing headers to be formatted with proper case (capitalize first letter of each word) as opposed to the lowercase normalization required internally.\n\nThe header validation and utility functions in `source/common/http/header_utility.cc` and `source/common/http/header_utility.h` provide helper functions like `isConnect()`, `requestHeadersValid()`, and `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` that support the header translation process by ensuring headers conform to HTTP specifications."
